posts.author: Mazeflare
posts.body: Predictions: 7 of 13 Winners, 1 Perfect, 390 Points | Tied for 4195th Alright, I've tried as hard as I could, and this is all I could find on MBS:https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC7nm7olVl1HqnGalF0ULkpwShe has been working closely with this guy who works for Fighting Nerds but he does a lot of individual trainings too, he is coaching Ruffy (who officially left the team) and a couple of other athletes, never heard of him but he is giving me strong David Goggins vibes, but this answer a question that has been plaguing me, does MBS still wants this? And the answer is yes, and she is trying, but to me, that is not enough, surrounding yourself with a good team its key, and I'm seeing her with a lot of randoms, a jiu-jitsu guy and a lot of other people I've never heard, its like she is doing her own thing instead of trusting a team. Very puzzling decision on a sport that heavily relies on others.I've did all this digging because I know full well Montague, I've been following since her first PFL fight, and I know her weaknesses too, and in my assessment, a dialed-in MBS can absolutely beat her and I hate to admit that. Still haven't made my mind because I have a weird feeling on this fight, Montague is working with Parrumpa which is a major green flag to me, he is an elite coach that is not in the media a lot but the knowledge he imparts on his athletes (Moicano, Pantoja, Arman, Bia Mesquita) especially on grapplers with his damage-first rule is second to none, and despite all of this, I can't help but to think that MBS is not completely out of this fight.

posts.author: Tazzmann85
posts.body: Predictions: 5 of 13 Winners, 4 Perfect, 365 Points | Tied for 4778th Sterling vs Zalal has potential to be a snoozefest and I’m actually not to keen for this fight as a main event or just the match up in general. Zalal in my eyes has always been a very talented fighter and I really believe he has the skill to be champion, crazy that he is still only 29 ! Probably one of the more technically sound guys at 145 too. He’s Clearly fixed up the wrestling holes he had previously and definitely worked on his overall strength. As good as Sterling is with his takedowns, top pressure I think he will struggle to get Zalal down let alone contain him. As I said I sort of see this being a boring fight with both guys engaging very little on the feet waiting to counter on one another, Zalal is pretty good at this type of fight. Think he does just enough on the feet to take the decision.Would rather have seen Zalal vs Jean. Jean Silva would take the fight to Zalal and this would be a super technical striking battle, I think we see this in the future when Jean Silva becomes champ

posts.author: Foxyy
posts.body: Predictions: 6 of 13 Winners, 6 Perfect, 475 Points | Tied for 2324th Buchecha vs. SpannMarcus Buchecha, SUB, R2I think Spann wins r1 and then Curtis Blaydes is the most dominant fundamental wrestler the division has ever seen, possessing a double-leg takedown so explosive and technically sound that it feels like an inevitability rather than a strike. Blaydes doesn't just compete; he imposes a grueling, high-pressure pace that suffocates opponents, blending world-class grappling with a vastly improved striking game. His ability to chain wrestling sequences together while maintaining the gas tank of a middleweight makes him a nightmare matchup for anyone in the world, proving time and again that he is the ultimate "gatekeeper to the gold" who consistently shuts down the hype of rising contenders. Beyond the raw physicality, there is a profound level of resilience and tactical intelligence in Blaydes’ game. He has navigated one of the toughest schedules in UFC history, never shying away from the most dangerous knockout artists in the sport. While others might play it safe, Blaydes trusts in his "Razor" elbows and his ability to neutralize chaos with calculated control. He is a true professional who stays out of the drama and lets his work inside the Octagon do the talking. Whether he’s setting records for the most takedowns in heavyweight history or showing off his evolved kickboxing, Blaydes remains a perennial title threat and a masterclass in how to weaponize elite wrestling in MMA.

posts.author: Belal Sucks
posts.body: Predictions: 8 of 13 Winners, 6 Perfect, 590 Points | Tied for 456th García vs. HernandezRafa García, DECI just got a gut feeling about this one here. Something strange was definitely going on to call off Hernandez’s last fight and I think at the very least that’s a distraction that he’s dealing with in preparation for this fight. The fight itself is a pretty tough matchup too considering the pressure and pace of Garcia to go against Hernandez’s patient and counter heavy “sniper” approach. I think honestly even though Hernandez has the better skills I have to go Rafa here soley for his minute winning ability in this matchup. Hernandez is either going to have to rely on a big knockout or resort to grappling if we wants to win this fight and I just don’t see either happening.Buchecha vs. SpannRyan Spann, TKO, R1Been kind of back and forth with this one. Ultimately I’m picking Spann because Buchecha hasn’t won a UFC fight yet and this is arguably his toughest test. Ryan Spann has every reason to win this fight but the problem is that he’s Ryan Spann. He’s about as inconsistent as they come so a matchup like this feels like there’s a wide possibility of outcomes. I’d be more worried if Buchecha had big power to go with his grappling but since he doesn’t I think Spann is going to be in the pocket swinging heavy.Bueno Silva vs. MontagueMichelle Montague, DECBueno Silva was never good and her somehow fluking her way to a title fight will always be funny to me. I’m gonna take Montague but on a side note I don’t think she’s all that either. She seems a bit green on the feet and she’s not exactly running through these girls with her grappling like Bia Mesquite. Still, the division needs some life so after she scoops up an easy win here I could see a big push coming.Filho vs. DurdenJafel Filho, SUB, R2Durden…. What are we doing buddy? Either take a fight with a full camp or just hang it up. Signing yourself up for these matchups where you have zero chance is making me think he’s just collecting a couple checks on his way out.Marshall vs. BrennanFrancis Marshall, SUB, R1Another layup for Marshall I suppose.Griffin vs. ValenzuelaMax Griffin, DECI’m going with the crafty vet here. He’s definitely not what he used to be which granted he was always pretty average but I like him here. Guys who are 0-1 on contender serries are typically a must fade and Valenzuela might just be a cool name merchant. If Griffin was a can I’d probably take the younger guy but hes always been kind of decent and that’s probably enough to get it done here.Alencar vs. PolastriJulia Polastri, DECKind of an interesting matchup. Not super high on either woman but it’s a classic striker vs grappler matchup which always makes for a compelling fight. I think I like Plostri’s ability to do more damage and be the aggressor here. I could see a possible mat smushing by Alencar but from the small sample size I’ve seen from her she doesn’t seem to have a great gas tank.

posts.body: Predictions: 5 of 13 Winners, 4 Perfect, 365 Points | Tied for 4778th Max Griffin vs Victor ValenzuelaGriffin has been in the UFC forever, he’s quite a limited fighter as far as skill, but he does everything ok and doesn’t have any glaring weaknesses apart from death gassing a few times in fights. Moves around a lot on the feet, ok jab and will go for a takedown in nearly all of his fights at least once. Always been a middle to lower in the pack in the UFC.Valenzuela himself isn’t talented or special either, decent stand up and has some power, at 5”9 he’s always going to be at a disadvantage at 170 with the reach. I like Victor here, I think Griffin may have a little success early with his jab and Victor will struggle to land on him early, but as the fight goes on Victor should land more as Griffin’s movement reduces on the feet. Victor could get the finish as Griffin is 40 now, but I’ve always found Griffin to handle himself pretty well whenever he gets hurt, even when he’s gassed as **** he still manages to keep his defences up and just survive in there. Only way I see Griffin taking this is if Valenzuela doesn’t go after Griffin and he lets Griffin play his game by staying on the outside throwing single strikes and not coming forward and pressuring. Valenzuela via Decision

posts.author: mick420
posts.body: Predictions: 4 of 13 Winners, 3 Perfect, 300 Points | Tied for 5966th It’s honestly embarrassing that people are still wasting their weekends on that Paramount UFC slop when WrestleMania just proved it’s the objectively superior MMA organization. While Dana White’s roster is busy hugging each other on the floor for three rounds, WWE fighters are out here landing 450 splashes and devastating powerbombs that would literally knock a UFC heavyweight unconscious in seconds. The lack of innovation in the Octagon is staggering—every "fighter" has the same buzzcut and uses the same recycled leg kicks, whereas WWE fighters are actually unique individuals who bring innovative, high-risk maneuvers to the ring that require way more technical skill than a standard jab. It’s a total no-brainer that the strikers in the WWE have more raw power and actually finish fights instantly instead of waiting for a judge’s scorecard. If you actually care about elite combat sports, you’d realize that these fighters are physically dominant specimens compared to the interchangeable mid-tier roster the UFC keeps pushing. Imagine thinking a "takedown" is more effective than a literal Tombstone Piledriver; the "Ultimate" league is just pure cope for people who can't handle the intensity of a truly innovative heavyweight clash.

posts.author: connorbrown16
posts.body: Predictions: 5 of 13 Winners, 5 Perfect, 375 Points | Tied for 4586th Talita Alencar vs. Julia Polastri Julia Polastri via Decision I’m not uber confident in this one for obvious WMMA reasons but Polastri is younger and bigger than Alencar who I think is a bit too one dimensional. Polastri just needs to keep this standing and avoid the clinch and she should take the decision victory. Max Griffin vs. Victor Valenzuela Victor Valenzuela via Decision Max Griffin’s 40? Damn, well this one probably won’t go his way either. Valenzuela is one of the rare “Lose your Contender Series bout but get signed anyway” guys so I don’t have much as far as expectations for him. I don’t see him finishing Griffin who’s still tough but has seen better days. The newcomer takes the Decision. Francis Marshall vs. Lucas Brennan Francis Marshall via Decision This isn’t going to be close because Marshall is going to dog walk Brennan from pillar to post. There’s something in the air tonight and it’s Decision wins. Jafel Filho vs. Cody Durden Jafel Filho via 1st Round Submission Fighter Removed: Cody Durden. Enough. Mayra Bueno Silva vs. Michelle Montague Michelle Montague via Decision Fighter Removed: Mayra Bueno Silva. Congrats on the new kid tho. Enjoy the family life. Sedriques Dumas vs. Jackson McVey Sedriques Dumas via Decision ****** meme fight that hopefully delivers on entertainment. I can’t pick the Alice In Chains roadie and Sedriques is so **** but atleast he’s athletic enough to compensate for his ****tiness. Just get this one over with as soon as possible. Dumbass take the Decision. Rodolfo Vieira vs. Eric McConico Rodolfo Vieira via 1st Round Submission McConico isn’t good whatsoever and it’s a matter of when and not if when it comes to Rodolfo getting the finish. I have little faith in McConico’s grappling defense so I’ll take Vieira to get the early Sub. Marcus Buchecha vs. Ryan Spann Marcus Buchecha via 2nd Round Submission The rare back-to-back submission finishes on a bleh card to spice things up. We’re doing the Fortis Fade because I also don’t trust Spann’s grappling defense especially if he gets gassed hunting for a finish and fails early. Buchecha finishes him with a 2nd round Kimura. Montel Jackson vs. Raoni Barcelos Montel Jackson via Decision Montel knows he needs to redeem himself after some lackluster performances but Barcelos is on a tear right now in his late 30’s. If Montel can work behind his jab and kicks he can keep Barcelos at bay in the striking exchanges. He’s also my dawg but this is a fight he needs if he wants to keep his slot near the Top 10 in the rankings so I’ll take him to get the hard fought decision nod. Davey Grant vs. Adrian Luna Martinetti Davey Grant via Decision You can’t teach an old dog new tricks but Davey is a battle tested pitbull who’s still able to bring it. Martinetti is like the rest of last year’s DWCS contract winners, you gotta prove it to me first before I’m a believer. I know what I’m getting in Davey and he’s going to teach the newbie there’s level to this and wins a decision that could be FOTN. Rafa Garcia vs. Alexander Hernandez Alexander Hernandez via 1st Round TKO Finally, somebody’s getting knocked out in this one. Hernandez has revitalized his career and is much more dangerous on the feet than Rafa. First round head kick TKO for El Gran Chango. Norma Durmont vs. Joselyne Edwards Norma Durmont via Decision And we’re back to 15 minute fights and this fight does nothing for me. Never been high on Edwards and Norma is a contender (I guess) at 135 so I’ll take the better fighter to outclass Edwards for the distance. Aljamain Sterling vs. Youssef Zalal Aljamain Sterling via Decision So glad this card is over with. Zalal’s hot streak comes to a halt as Aljo is just more experienced in 5 round fights and looks more comfortable at 145 than I would have expected. Zalal really needs to stick-n-move one the outside but he’ll only be able to do that for so long until Aljo will be able to control him and he just won’t be able to fend off the takedowns and pressure. Aljo takes the…Decision and that’s all folks.

posts.author: ChrisSweeney
posts.body: Predictions: 6 of 13 Winners, 3 Perfect, 405 Points | Tied for 3843rd Sterling vs. ZalalYoussef Zalal, DECLow confidence pick, but I think Zalal is catching Aljo at the right time. He's not washed but he’s declining and Zalal has the right tools for it. I’m very suspicious of Aljo after the Ortega performance. It’s not a good look that someone hospitalized days before the fight requests a 5 rounder to gas you out. It was a slow paced fight where Aljo didn’t even shoot for the first two rounds and still gassed. This fight is gonna have to be a wrestling heavy gameplan which is worrying. Zalal’s cardio could be very useful here. Zalal didn’t gas out in the Katter performance. I think he just took the round off. I think he’s gonna look good in the championship rounds in his first 5 rounder. While this is a huge jump for Zalal, I'm gonna forget about Aljo’s name value and think of these two actually fighting. Aljo needs pressure to close the distance takedown and control Zalal. Zalal has great lateral movement and footwork to counter this, He also has the clear advantage in the striking and has knees he regularly throws up the middle. But it’s not a striker vs. grappler match-up. Zalal can hold himself on the ground against Aljo too, He’s definitely the more dangerous guy on the mat. I don’t think Aljo is gonna dominate him with the control or finish the guy who’s never been finished as a pro. Zalal by a kinda boring technical decision. 3x 48-47 Zalal.Dumont vs. EdwardsNorma Dumont, DECGood WMMA fight. But there’s levels. Yeah Joselyne is on a nice 4 fight finish streak. But her best win out of it is Nora Cornolle where she looked awful until the injury on the slam. I backed her confidently but she disappointed me, So now to me she’s back to her old self. Her boxing is sloppy but aggressive and she’s struggled with good boxers before, Cornolle was winning the striking in her last fight too. Dumont is a technical boring decision merchant, she’s low-risk but she's at a high level for WMMA when it comes to striking. Dumont got controlled by Vieira in her last fight but her TDD and ground game aren’t non-existent, Vieira just is a high level WMMA grappler. Joselyne’s takedowns aren’t amazing either; she kinda fails takedowns until she finally gets one. If Joselyne comes out wrestle heavy and finds success it could get interesting but I think Dumont's skill and variety on the feet ends Joselyne’s nice run. 3x 30-27 Dumont.García vs. HernandezAlexander Hernandez, TKO, R2Re-155 Hernandez looks great. I think Hernandez keeps his streak going here. This is a good match-up for him to win. I think his style plays perfectly into this fight and he has multiple key advantages. Footwork, speed, power, and athleticism. Hernandez is always moving and exploding in and out of range to land big shots with his real power. For a guy like Garcia who’s slow and does his best work standing there in the pocket, along with the fact he’s not athletic or a huge power puncher. I don’t see his striking being that effective here. I don’t think he can catch Hernandez and wrestle him, or pressure and try to gas Hernandez. Jared Gordon also rocked Garcia many times in their fight where Gordon was coming in off a car crash. Garcia can’t eat too many clean punches, Hernandez has the power to put his lights out off a counter.Grant vs. Luna MartinettiDavey Grant, DECThis is gonna get crafty. This a hard ass debut for Luna Martinetti I don’t think he’s bad but there’s really levels. The only reason Davey Grant isn’t a lock is because of his age. If Davey can keep it standing his boxing will cause big problems for Martinetti. Watching Martinetti just eat all those punches from Vologdin, he literally just can’t do that here. And Vologdin is by far the best opponent Luna Martinetti has gone against. With his size advantage he still went life or death with a 125’er who just got punked on by Castaneda. And the rest of Martinetti’s record is fighters with a losing record. He doesn't have much power, He’s super hittable, And I don’t think he’s controlling Davey like that. If he gets the takedown he doesn’t do much damage he’s not gonna finish Davey on the ground without some damage and Davey is crafty himself. Obviously Davey has the experience and is crafty as one can be, at 40 he still looks in shape. He still has his speed, durability, and power. Grant is a great boxer and an unusual striker who throws powerful, fast hooks from odd angles and mixes in a lot of kicks and crafty moves. I don’t think he’s near the level of losing to mid debutants. 3x 30-27 Grant. The finish is live.Jackson vs. BarcelosRaoni Barcelos, DECMaybe I love 40 year olds, but I think Barcelos wins this too. Barcelos finally gets his shot at the rankings, he's in the best form of his career, an amazing career comeback and this is long awaited. Montel just lost his momentum and the inactivity is catching up. Montel is supposed to be this dark horse of the division with crazy one shot power, a crazy reach, and elite wrestling. And while he does have one shot power and a crazy reach he’s a no-output striker who can’t pull the trigger and freezes up (Yes I picked Figgy). And I have no idea where “elite wrestling” came from; he's been out-wrestled in all his losses. Montel can definitely catch Barcelos but how does just that make him a favorite? If he doesn’t catch him he’s completely cooked. And only Umar has caught Barcelos so far. Montel fights like a sparring session at range, Barcelos fights at 10x the pace of Montel, and is a way better boxer and throws a lot fast and will take away that range for his takedowns. Which Barcelos, the 5-time Brazilian National Wrestling Champion and 2-time South American Freestyle Champion can shoot over and over again making the already passive Montel more passive. 3x 30-27 Barcelos.Buchecha vs. SpannRyan Spann, TKO, R1It’s hard to pick either of these guys, Spann is also on short notice. I guess I’m gonna pick the fighter who’s at least done something in the UFC. On the feet, Spann is a big athletic striker who has the clear advantage in the striking. He hits harder and is more dangerous in exchanges. but he’s very inconsistent, and has poor fighting IQ. Buchecha the BJJ nerd has literally done nothing in the UFC but lose to Buday and Nzechukwu. Nzechukwu got taken down by the worst takedown i’ve ever seen and got insta-subbed but BJJ prodigy Buchecha couldn’t do anything with 3 takedowns. Spann should win this, if he pulls a guillotine I swear to god.Vieira vs. McConicoRodolfo Vieira, SUB, R2Early UFC Rodolfo Vieira is what Buchecha should also be. The key issue for Viera is whether he can reliably get takedowns. In recent matchups against fighters with decent TDD, he struggled to consistently control opponents once he did get them down, and his entries looked slow. His striking is also limited, mostly relying on a simple jab that doesn’t carry much power or threat. The good thing for him is that McConico isn’t much; he's a NPC fighter. He’s not special anywhere, but he has good length, decent striking, solid cardio, and basic takedown defense. His biggest weakness is lack of output, but he has the advantage on the feet here. Simply put I think Vieira does find the takedown eventually and when he does his BJJ is high level and proven enough to get McConico out of there.Dumas vs. McVeyJackson McVey, TKO, R1McVey is gonna dominate this fight in the clinch. He was hurting Reese badly before the takedown and he got deleted. Dumas is just an awful meme fighter who has been kept around for whatever reason. He has very limited striking, weak grappling, poor cardio, and can’t handle any pressure or anyone that will fight back. Even his wins haven’t been convincing. At least McVey has some advantages. He’s bigger, more aggressive, and trains at a better camp now. He’s still raw, especially at range and in grappling, but he can pressure opponents, work in the clinch, and land knees and elbows that can cause damage. McVey should win this slop-fest.Bueno Silva vs. MontagueMichelle Montague, DECMontague is a good WMMA prospect, while it’s crazy MBS is getting another fight. She’s on a bad 4 fight losing-streak and looks checked-out. When MBS is taken down, she stays on her back without trying to get up. She stays her guard and doesn’t do enough from it. If Montague gets even one takedown, she’ll likely control the entire round from the top. Montague is a very physical grappler and trains consistently with high-level grapplers at ATT. She should make this look easy if she’s a serious fighter. 3x 30-27 Montague.Filho vs. DurdenJafel Filho, SUB, R1This is an obvious show-out for Filho. Short notice Cody Durden against a dangerous grappler. I backed Durden in his last one and I can say I'll never do it again. Coming off a really poor performance where he looked tired quickly, shot awful takedowns, and was repeatedly controlled and reversed against a mid Tumendeberel. It’s proven Durden is on a downwards spiral. His cardio is bad, his wrestling has regressed, his striking is unreliable, and he makes bad decisions on the ground. He also struggles a lot defensively against submissions. Filho is a strong grappler with dangerous submission skills and solid takedowns. Once the fight hits the mat, he’s very capable from both top and bottom, but dominant when he gets top position or the back. He has strong control and is dangerous in submissions from anywhere any grappling exchange will go Filho’s way. If Durden shoots, he risks being submitted or reversed. If he tries to strike, he’s at risk of getting hurt or taken down anyway. Filho gets the submission early.Marshall vs. BrennanFrancis Marshall, TKO, R3Francis is a good MMA fighter with potential who’s impressed me recently. Walked through Erik Silva not that long ago, and arguably beat Mairon Santos. I have no idea why I wouldn’t pick him here against a short notice debutant.Griffin vs. ValenzuelaMax Griffin, DECValenzuela Getting beat by my favorite DWCS prospect Michael Oliveira is not a huge deal, I think that loss will age fine. But I’m gonna pick the durable UFC veteran with some skills. Even though he’s hard to trust because he’s 40 and does slow down after round one. But Griffen’s the cleaner striker, has more experience, and can mix in wrestling if he chooses. Valenzuela is very aggressive but sloppy, gets hit a lot, and is willing to brawl. There’s a real chance Griffin hurts him early and even if he fades he can slide by with a decision. I don’t think Valenzuela is gonna be the guy to KO Griffin or give him a three fight losing streak. Griffin via split decision.Alencar vs. PolastriJulia Polastri, DECPick’em WMMA fight. If Alencar gets takedowns, she can win, but if Polastri keeps it standing and applies pressure, she will win. Polastri is dangerous on the feet. She’s aggressive, throws a lot, and has good cardio. If she keeps the fight standing, she should clearly outstrike Alencar who can’t strike. The big concern is Polastri’s grappling. If she gets taken down, she just stays on her back and doesn’t fight to get up. which could cost her the fight since Alencar has good top control. Alencar has improved, but she’s still pretty average overall. Her last two wins came against opponents with no takedown defense, so it’s hard to take much from them. I imagine Polastri keeps it mostly standing and wins a decision. 29-28 Polastri. * Edited at 04.25.2026, 4:13 PM ET *

posts.author: battousai
posts.body: Predictions: 5 of 13 Winners, 3 Perfect, 325 Points | Tied for 5501st Sterling vs. ZalalAljamain Sterling, DECZalal may be able to take one or two of the early rounds but I have to lean on Aljos experience here all day. Zalal hasn't really shown me much on this recent run other than he clearly belongs in the rankings. Aljo is no spring chicken anymore but I've been seeing people say all week that he is washed up and cooked which is just bonkers. He gave Movsar his toughest test yet (up until he fought Lerone) and held up an insane pace with his grappling in that one. He then flew halfway around the world and whopped on Ortega in a fight that probably shouldn't have even happened. Not sure Aljo needed to do much to win that one much in the way that many argue Zalal didn't need to do much and "coasted" his way through a 3rd round with Kattar. I think Aljo will pull ahead as this fight goes on and dominate the championship rounds. Funkmaster 48-47.Dumont vs. EdwardsNorma Dumont, DECI'm very tempted to be contrarian and take Edwards here but to be honest, Dumont looked absolutely phenomenal on the scales. She looks shredded and is in tremendous shape. She typically relies on just using her volume to win fights which is why this fight is so close. Edwards will fire just as much back at her and probably has more power but Dumonts very, very hard to hit. While she lacks finishing ability and is kind of boring the reality is she's above average striker striker who just relies on continuous volume and above average defense. Both will work in the odd takedown which is where I think the difference in this fight might be. Whoever can get their grappling game going first might be the one who eventually is able to pull ahead.García vs. HernandezAlexander Hernandez, DECThis fight is very close. If Hernandez can keep this fight mostly on the feet I think he will win and might even find a late finish. Rafa isn't a super powerful guy so I doubt he finishes Hernandez and will likely have to rely on winning minutes here with his grappling. I don't think I will ever feel any type of confidence picking Hernandez because he's historically always been kind of streaky but he might have finally hit his stride recently.Grant vs. Luna MartinettiDavey Grant, SUB, R2Grant is going to club and sub this bum.Jackson vs. BarcelosMontel Jackson, TKO, R1Speed kills and more and more of it disappears with age.Buchecha vs. SpannMarcus Buchecha, SUB, R1A 3rd sub in a row? Sure, why not. In all seriousness, heavyweight Ryan Spann looks disgusting. Hard for me to take this guy seriously with how he looks considering he was beating ranked LHW's at one point. He literally just looks like a fat blob who rolled off the coach to come fight. I think my jaw was almost on the floor when they announced he came in just under the limit. He has progressively just gotten fatter and fatter since moving up and this is RYAN SPANN we're talking about. It was hard to take this guy seriously sometimes even when he was at least in decent shape. Buchecha is more well rounded then he is given credit for and has looked ok so far in the UFC. This is probably the easiest fight he's been given so far and I have no issue taking the better athlete who at least on paper appears to be taking his career seriously.Vieira vs. McConicoRodolfo Vieira, SUB, R1To be honest, not very confident in this pick. McConico is not good but Vieiras last out was a terrible look. It wasn't a terrible performance overall especially considering the talent and potential Nickal has but the way he faded in that fight was rough to watch. He has never had a good gas tank but he was so hurt and exhausted in that 3rd round that Nickal honestly could have finished him however he wanted. That was a brutal knockout that has probably compromised an already not great chin. Unfortunately for McConico, he's just not dangerous enough himself for me to have any confidence in taking him here. He will have some reach but Vieira's striking is leaps and bounds better compared to when he first got to the UFC. Outside of McConico landing a lucky shot and compromising Vieira early I think this is his fight to lose. These two guys will go back and forth on the feet but once this fight goes pretty much anywhere else Vieira should have a massive advantage. The miles he's wearing and the durability concerns worry me, but McConico is old himself and a jobber level talent. This fight very much feels like a reward for taking a tough fight against one of the promotions shiny new toys.Dumas vs. McVeyJackson McVey, TKO, R2To be completely honest, I think Jackson McVey sucks ass and should not be in the UFC yet. However, I can't even say Dumas is ass. I don't consider him a serious fighter and will never pick him. Period. This fight is going to be absolutely terrible/hilarious for as long as it lasts, which hopefully isn't very long. With both guys fighting for their jobs I could see the first round being a bit less chaotic than most are expecting but I hope I'm wrong.Bueno Silva vs. MontagueMichelle Montague, DECTorch passing fight. Montague is so much better overall than Silva its not even funny. One trick pony being taken out to pasture.Filho vs. DurdenJafel Filho, SUB, R3Durden is a dog and will put up a good fight but Pastor is the best unranked flyweight in the promotion at present if you ask me. The longer this fight stays on the feet the longer Durden will stay in it but as a wrestler I just see him probably getting stuck in a bad position at some point and eventually getting subbed.Marshall vs. BrennanFrancis Marshall, DECI'm not as high on Marshall as some people are but Brennan is a bum. Don't see him struggling here whatsoever and I think this fight being short notice up a weight class will benefit him because he's the stronger, better athlete.Griffin vs. ValenzuelaMax Griffin, TKO, R3Griffin is way more experienced, longer, and is fighting a short notice guy who's taking his 2nd fight in 23 days.Alencar vs. PolastriJulia Polastri, DECSmol fight.
